items.headline: Singapore's landed home market slows in 2026 but sellers still hold the upper hand
items.summary: Landed home transactions have declined in 2026, but tight supply means sellers can hold firm on prices, keeping it a sellers' market despite the quieter activity. Buyers face fewer choices and less room to negotiate.
items.meaning: If you are hoping for a price correction before buying a landed home, limited supply makes a meaningful drop unlikely in the near term.

items.headline: 32-year-old Kallang/Whampoa executive HDB flat sells for record $1.412 million
items.summary: A 32-year-old executive HDB flat in Kallang/Whampoa changed hands at $1.412 million, setting a new resale record for the town. The sale shows that older large HDB flats in central locations can still command top dollar.
items.meaning: HDB sellers in similar mature estates should know their flats can still fetch record prices even as the units age.

items.headline: Condo resale prices rebound in May 2026 even as fewer units change hands
items.summary: Condo resale prices climbed in May 2026 despite transaction volumes easing, suggesting buyers continue to pay up for the right units. The uptick follows a softer patch and points to underlying demand holding firm.
items.meaning: If you are waiting for condo resale prices to dip before buying, the market appears to have stabilised and is moving up again.

items.headline: URA releases two new condo land sites in Bright Hill and Farrer Park, with Farrer Park backed by years of pent-up demand
items.summary: Two new Government Land Sale sites for condo development have been released in Bright Hill and Farrer Park, with the Farrer Park plot expected to draw especially keen developer bids given years of limited new supply in that area. Completed projects will add fresh condo inventory to both neighbourhoods within a few years.
items.meaning: Buyers and owners near Farrer Park should know that new condo supply is coming, which could affect resale values and rental competition when those projects eventually launch.

items.headline: June 2026 BTO ballot: Bishan and Woodlands see strong demand
items.summary: Application rates for the June 2026 BTO exercise show Bishan and Woodlands attracting notably strong interest, with those towns among the most competitive choices. Buyers chasing popular locations will face steep odds.
items.meaning: If you are balloting in June 2026, choosing a less popular town can dramatically improve your chances of actually getting a flat sooner.

items.headline: 38-year-old Bukit Timah maisonette sets new town record at $1.508 million
items.summary: A 38-year-old HDB maisonette in Bukit Timah sold for $1,508,888, breaking the town's previous resale record. Demand for large HDB units near good schools remains strong regardless of flat age.
items.meaning: Owners of older HDB maisonettes in sought-after towns still have real pricing power if they decide to sell.
